title: Federal dividend tax credit – Personal income tax - Canada.ca
content:
## Federal dividend tax credit – Personal income tax
- Provincial or territorial: Line 61520
- Tax year: 2025 | Prior tax years: 2025 and earlier
You may be able to claim the federal dividend tax credit if you reported dividend income from taxable Canadian corporations on your income tax and benefit return.
- Who can claim the federal dividend tax credit
- How much you can claim

## Who can claim the federal dividend tax credit
Generally, you can claim the federal dividend tax credit if you meet all of the following conditions:
- You received dividends from taxable Canadian corporations (foreign dividends do not qualify for this credit)
- You reported these dividends on your income tax return on: Line 12000 (eligible dividends) Line 12010 (other than eligible dividends)
- Line 12000 (eligible dividends)
- Line 12010 (other than eligible dividends)

## How much you can claim
The amount you can claim depends on:
- the type of dividend (eligible or other than eligible)
- the taxable amount of the dividends reported on your income tax return

### Calculate your dividend tax credit
- Federal amount for 2025 Your federal dividend tax credit is the total of the dividend tax credit amounts shown on your information slips: T3 slips (boxes 39 and 51) T4PS slips (boxes 26 and 32) T5 slips (boxes 12 and 26) T5013 slips (boxes 131 and 134) If you did not receive an information slip for any of the dividends that you received, complete the chart for line 40425 on your Federal Worksheet to calculate your claim.

### Federal amount for 2025
Your federal dividend tax credit is the total of the dividend tax credit amounts shown on your information slips:
- T3 slips (boxes 39 and 51)
- T4PS slips (boxes 26 and 32)
- T5 slips (boxes 12 and 26)
- T5013 slips (boxes 131 and 134)
If you did not receive an information slip for any of the dividends that you received, complete the chart for line 40425 on your Federal Worksheet to calculate your claim.
- Provincial and territorial amounts for 2025 The dividend tax credit amount you can claim may also vary depending on the province or territory where you lived at the end of the year : Alberta Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet AB428 to calculate your claim. British Columbia Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet BC428 to calculate your claim. Manitoba Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet MB428 to calculate your claim. New Brunswick Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NB428 to calculate your claim. Newfoundland and Labrador Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NL428 to calculate your claim. Northwest Territories Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NT428 to calculate your claim. Nova Scotia Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NS428 to calculate your claim. Nunavut Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NU428 to calculate your claim. Ontario Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet ON428 to calculate your claim. Prince Edward Island Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet PE428 to calculate your claim. Quebec Refer to: Revenu Québec Saskatchewan Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet SK428 to calculate your claim. Yukon Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet YT428 to calculate your claim.

### Provincial and territorial amounts for 2025
The dividend tax credit amount you can claim may also vary depending on the province or territory where you lived at the end of the year :
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet AB428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet BC428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet MB428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NB428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NL428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NT428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NS428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet NU428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet ON428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet PE428 to calculate your claim.
Refer to: Revenu Québec
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet SK428 to calculate your claim.
Complete the chart for line 61520 on Worksheet YT428 to calculate your claim.

## How to claim this credit

### Claim the federal dividend tax credit on your federal tax return and Form 428
- Federal tax return On line 40425 of your federal tax return, enter the federal dividend tax credit amount that you determined under How much you can claim .
On line 40425 of your federal tax return, enter the federal dividend tax credit amount that you determined under How much you can claim .
- Provincial or territorial Form 428 On line 61520 of your provincial or territorial Form 428 , enter the provincial or territorial dividend tax credit amount that you determined under How much you can claim .

#### Provincial or territorial Form 428
On line 61520 of your provincial or territorial Form 428 , enter the provincial or territorial dividend tax credit amount that you determined under How much you can claim .

## Forms and publications
- Lines 12000 and 12010 – Taxable amount of dividends from taxable Canadian corporations
